reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Verified code reading at eip7594.c:875-884. The bug is real: compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge() is called with commitments_bytes (original array) and num_commitments (count of unique commitments after deduplication at line 859). When duplicates exist, the hash includes the first num_commitments elements from the ORIGINAL array instead of the deduplicated unique_commitments array. This creates challenge malleability where different orderings of the same cryptographic content (e.g., [A,B,A,C] vs [A,B,C,A]) produce different Fiat-Shamir challenges despite both deduplicating to [A,B,C]. The 01e requirement (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json) explicitly states: 'Fiat-Shamir challenge hash must include all verifier-relevant inputs' with assertion requiring hash_input to contain 'comms'. The verifier-relevant commitments are the unique set, not a prefix of the original array. This violates Fiat-Shamir soundness and enables selective forgery attacks. Severity calibrated to High: c-kzg-4844 is used by multiple Ethereum clients (library-level bug), affecting >33% of network if multiple clients deploy the vulnerable version, enabling batch proof forgery via challenge malleability.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: 01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json: 'Fiat-Shamir challenge hash must include all verifier-relevant inputs to prevent selective forgery.'
